Fool’s Gold (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $70,231,041Details
International Box Office $39,131,925
Worldwide Box Office $109,362,966
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $21,306,089
Total $21,306,089
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Treasure hunter Ben "Finn" Finnegan has sunk his marriage to Tess (Kate Hudson) and his trusty boat in his obsessive quest to find the legendary Queen's Dowry. When he finds a vital clue that may finally pinpoint the treasure's whereabouts, he drags Tess and her boss, billionaire Nigel Honeycutt, along on the hunt. But Finn is not the only one interested in the gold; his former mentor-turned-enemy Moe Fitch will stop at nothing to beat him to it.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$21,589,295 (30.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.25 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$72,500,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,125 opening theaters/3,125 max. theaters, 6.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $116,986,525

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 8th, 2008 (Wide) by Warner Bros., released as Fool's Gold
Video Release: June 17th, 2008 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for action violence, some sexual material, brief nudity and language
Running Time: 112 minutes
Plot point: Exes in Love, Kidnap, Money Troubles, Relationships Gone Wrong, Rescue, Treasure Hunters, Underwater
Social setting: Hip Hop and Rap
Subgenre: Chase Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., De Line Pictures
Production Countries: United States, Australia
Languages: English

Synopsis

Treasure hunter Ben "Finn" Finnegan has sunk his marriage to Tess (Kate Hudson) and his trusty boat in his obsessive quest to find the legendary Queen's Dowry. When he finds a vital clue that may finally pinpoint the treasure's whereabouts, he drags Tess and her boss, billionaire Nigel Honeycutt, along on the hunt. But Finn is not the only one interested in the gold; his former mentor-turned-enemy Moe Fitch will stop at nothing to beat him to it.
